behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module data handoff (three collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary step), not single-function logic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires that the payload "includes data retrieved from all three sources" and does not require real network/OS/subprocess effects; rejected Agentic because it validates pre-summary input assembly, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/aggregation modules together with deterministic source adapters (fixtures), run collection, and assert the exact summary-input payload contains all three source sections/keys and no source is dropped during handoff.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/state propagation across modules: fetch failure handling -> section annotation -> report continuation. MOCK-MISS applies at module boundary, but REAL-INFRA is not required because a controlled failing adapter can reproduce the failure contract. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about fallback behavior ("data unavailable" note) rather than real network stack quirks; rejected Agentic because correctness here is control flow, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline with two normal source adapters and one adapter that raises the same exception class used for source-unavailable cases; assert process completes, output includes `data unavailable` for only that section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generation quality/faithfulness of the summary itself, which is model-output dependent and can vary; deterministic stubs would hide hallucination/omission failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly targets semantic accuracy ("accurately represents the key values"), which a mocked generator cannot validate.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary generation on a fixed dataset with known key values, run multiple trials, and evaluate factual consistency (all required key values present, no contradictory values). Fail on omissions/distortions beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires real external delivery boundary (email transport + inbox receipt), so real network/API/SMTP behavior is part of the failure mode. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because mocked email send can pass while real delivery fails (auth, routing, provider response handling); rejected Agentic because acceptance criterion is end-to-end delivery to inbox, not quality of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run against reachable test instances/services and a real test mailbox, then verify the mailbox receives exactly one report email for configured recipients with expected subject/body structure and attached/embedded report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real service unreachability semantics plus persistence across runs (local file + subsequent run retry), which commonly fails only with real I/O/network behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because a simple mock send failure can miss real unreachable-service error forms/timing and filesystem persistence edge cases; rejected Workflow because this is retry orchestration over runs, not multiple agentic model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with email endpoint intentionally unreachable (or blackholed test endpoint), assert report is durably written to configured local path; run next scheduled invocation with service restored, assert queued file is retried/sent and local pending artifact/state is cleared appropriately.
